title: Cleavage coupled competitive lateral flow assay
abstract: Disclosed herein is a test device for determining the quantity of a biologic in a sample comprising (a) a sample pad for receiving the biologic, (b) a conjugate pad comprising one or more peptide dimers and one or more antibodies conjugated to a conjugate reagent, and (c) a test membrane comprising at least one test line, the test line comprising an immobilized mimetope. Also disclosed herein is a method of determining the presence, quantity or potency of a biologic, the method comprising: (a) contacting the biologic with a test device, as described above, (b) determining whether at least one test line undergoes a color change, (c) determining, based on the color change, the presence, quantity or potency of the biologic. Further disclosed herein is a kit comprising test device, as described above, and instructions for use thereof.
claims: Claims CLAIMS  WHAT IS CLAIMED IS : 1. A device for determining the quantity of a biologic in a sample comprising (a) a sample pad for receiving the biologic, (b) a conjugate pad comprising one or more peptide dimers and one or more antibodies conjugated to a conjugate reagent, and (c) a test membrane comprising at least one test line, the test line comprising an immobilized mimetope. 2. The device of claim 1, wherein the test device is a lateral flow immunoassay. 3. The device of claim 1 wherein the biologic is selected from the group consisting of a protease, an endonuclease, an organic compound, a toxin, and an enzyme that cleaves a bond or dissociates an otherwise stable interaction. 4. The device of claim 3 wherein the toxin is selected from the group consisting of a neurotoxin, an exotoxin, and an endotoxin. 5. The device of claim 4 wherein the neurotoxin or the exotoxin is botulinum toxin. 6. The device of claim 5 wherein the botulinum toxin is Onabotulinumtoxin A (BOTOX). 7. The test device of claim 4 wherein the endotoxin is Lipopolysaccharide (LPS). 8. The device of claim 1, wherein the conjugate reagent is selected from the group consisting of colloidal gold, latex particles, enzymes, colored dyes, paramagnetic particles, and fluorescent particles. 9. The device of claim 1 wherein the sample pad further comprises a buffer, pH calibrator, peptide, or antibody. 10. The device of claim 1 wherein the biologic is present in a fluid. 11. The device of claim 10 wherein the fluid is an aqueous or non-aqueous fluid, or organic or non-organic fluid, or biological or non-biological fluid. 12. The device of claim 10, wherein the fluid is selected from the group consisting of a pharmaceutical composition, organic liquid waste, and inorganic liquid waste. 13. The device of claim 1, wherein conjugate pad further comprises recombinant Factor C (rFc). 14. The device of claim 1, wherein the test membrane comprises at least one test line and at least one control line. 15. The device of claim 14, wherein the at least one test line comprises an immobilized mimetope. 16. The device of claim 15, wherein the test membrane comprises more than one test line, and each test line contains an immobilized mimetope different from another test line. 17. The device of claim 13, wherein the at least one test line is upstream of the at least one control line. 18. The device of claim 13, wherein at least one control line comprises a light chain antibody. 19. The device of claim 18, wherein the light chain antibody is an anti-kappa light chain antibody or an anti-lambda light chain antibody. 20. The device of claim 13, wherein at least one control line comprises a secondary antibody that binds directly to the antibody conjugated to the conjugate reagent. 21. The device of claim 1, further comprising a wi eking pad. 22. A method of determining the presence, quantity or potency of a biologic, the method comprising: (a) contacting the biologic with a test device of any one of claims 1-21, (b) determining whether at least one test line undergoes a color change, and (c) determining, based on the color change, the presence, quantity or potency of the biologic. 23. A kit compri sing : (i) a test device of any one of claims 1-21, and (ii) instructions for use thereof. 24. The kit of claim 23, further comprising a biologic sample. 25. The kit of claim 23, further comprising a sample applicator. 26. A method of determining the presence, quantity or potency of a biologic, the method comprising: (a) contacting the biologic with a test device comprising: (i) a sample pad for receiving the biologic, (ii) a conjugate pad comprising one or more peptide dimers and one or more antibodies conjugated to a conjugate reagent, and (iii) a test membrane comprising at least one test line, the test line comprising an immobilized mimetope; (b) determining whether at least one test line undergoes a color change, and (c) determining, based on the color change, the presence, quantity or potency of the biologic, wherein the biologic is selected from the group consisting of Onabotulinumtoxin A (BOTOX) and Lipopolysaccharide (LPS).
